name: Tous Reservoir Loop from La Pobla Llarga
name_original: El Pantano de Tous — Circular desde La Pobla Llarga
description: This moderate 36.7-mile road cycling route from La Pobla Llarga offers scenic views of the Tous Reservoir and surrounding agricultural lands
region_intro: Road cycling routes around La Pobla Llarga traverse a diverse Mediterranean landscape, characterized by verdant orchards, quiet secondary roads, and varied terrain. The region offers both flat sections ideal for speed and challenging climbs leading into nearby natural parks like Sierra Calderona and Sierra de Espadán. Cyclists can explore historical elements and picturesque villages, with routes often utilizing rural roads between private farms. Best road cycling routes around La Pobla Llarga The most popular road cycling route is Tous Reservoir Loop from La Pobla Llarga, a 36.7 miles (59.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 52 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the reservoir and surrounding natural areas. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ateneo Sueco del Socorro – Cullera Lighthouse loop from Castelló, a moderate 64.4 miles (103.7 km) path. This route features coastal sections and views towards the lighthouse. Local road cyclists also love the La Visteta Viewpoint – Port de Barx loop from Castelló, a 45.5 miles (73.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 3 hours 33 minutes. Road cycling around La Pobla Llarga is defined by its Mediterranean landscape, proximity to natural parks, and extensive network of greenways. The region offers options for different ability levels, from gentle rides through orchards to demanding mountain passes. The routes in La Pobla Llarga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 6000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore La Pobla Llarga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around La Pobla Llarga?
A: There are over 100 road cycling routes around La Pobla Llarga, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. You'll find everything from gentle rides through orchards to challenging climbs in nearby natural parks.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near La Pobla Llarga?
A: The region offers a diverse Mediterranean landscape. You can expect smooth asphalt roads that traverse verdant orchards and quiet secondary roads. Routes feature both flat sections ideal for speed and more challenging climbs as you approach natural parks like Sierra Calderona and Sierra de Espadán.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in La Pobla Llarga?
A: Yes, La Pobla Llarga offers several routes suitable for less experienced cyclists or families. There are 37 easy routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e857215531/tous-reservoir-loop-from-la-pobla-llarga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tous Reservoir Loop from La Pobla Llarga&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option that provides beautiful views without extreme difficulty. Additionally, the Vía Verde Ribera Costera offers a gentler itinerary through the Valencian garden landscape.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 15 difficult routes around La Pobla Llarga. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31994667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sellent Viewpoint – Albufera de Anna loop from Castelló&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gains, reaching over 1100 meters. The proximity to natural parks like Sierra Calderona and Sierra de Espadán also means access to demanding mountain passes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling near La Pobla Llarga?
A: Road cycling routes often pass through picturesque villages and offer views of significant natural features. You might encounter the scenic Murta Valley, or cycle near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4758759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Murta Municipal Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also lead to viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/362227" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Creu del Cardenal&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Can I find long-distance road cycling routes around La Pobla Llarga?
A: Yes, the region provides options for longer rides. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31535351" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ateneo Sueco del Socorro – Cullera Lighthouse loop from Castelló&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 103.7 km path that includes coastal sections. The broader 'Route of the Monasteries' (GR-236) is also a 90-kilometer itinerary that connects several towns and historical sites, offering a great option for an extended ride.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Many of the road cycling routes around La Pobla Llarga are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e857215531/tous-reservoir-loop-from-la-pobla-llarga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tous Reservoir Loop from La Pobla Llarga&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39558427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port de Barx – La Visteta Viewpoint loop from La Pobla Llarga&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in La Pobla Llarga?
A: La Pobla Llarga, being in the Valencian Community, generally offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, with mild temperatures and less chance of extreme heat. Winter can also be suitable, though some mountain passes might be cooler. It's advisable to check local weather forecasts, especially during the hotter summer months.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in La Pobla Llarga?
A: The road cycling experience in La Pobla Llarga is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 60 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse Mediterranean landscape, the blend of flat sections and challenging climbs, and the opportunity to explore picturesque villages and historical sites on quiet secondary roads.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural points of interest accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, cycling routes in the area often pass through or near historical sites. The 'Route of the Monasteries' connects several monasteries of historical and artistic interest, such as the Convent of Santa María de Aguas Vivas. You can also cycle towards the impressive Castell de Xàtiva, a strategically located fortress, or explore the Vía Verde Ribera Costera which features historical elements like the church of Santa Ana.

Q: Are there options for road cyclists to find cafes or accommodation near the routes?
A: Yes, many routes pass through or near picturesque villages where you can find local cafes for a break. While specific accommodation details are not provided here, the presence of these villages and towns like La Pobla Llarga itself, and nearby Xàtiva, suggests that finding places to eat and stay for cyclists should be feasible.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to cycle the Tous Reservoir circular route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is designed for racebikes and generally takes about 2 hours and 53 minutes to complete. This duration is for cycling and does not include stops.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is rated as moderate. While much of the terrain in the Valencia region is paved, some sections, particularly those closer to the Tous Reservoir, might be unpaved or challenging. Road cyclists should be prepared for varied surfaces and a good level of fitness is recommended.

faqs.question: Is the Tous Reservoir circular route suitable for road bikes?
faqs.answer: While categorized for racebikes, it's important to note that some segments around the Tous Reservoir may be unpaved. Riders on road bikes should verify current surface conditions, as these sections might be more suitable for mountain or gravel bikes.

faqs.question: Where does the route start, and what are the parking options?
faqs.answer: The route starts from La Pobla Llarga. Parking options would typically be found within the town itself, though specific dedicated trailhead parking for this exact route is not detailed. Look for public parking areas in La Pobla Llarga.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse scenery, including agricultural areas like orchards, natural enclaves, and picturesque villages. The route often follows quiet secondary roads, offering scenic views of the Tous Reservoir and the surrounding natural landscape.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Valencian Community generally offers a favorable climate for cycling year-round. However, spring and autumn typically provide the most comfortable temperatures for a moderate ride like this, avoiding the peak heat of summer.

faqs.question: Are there any specific permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are mentioned or typically required for cycling this route around the Tous Reservoir.

faqs.question: Is the Tous Reservoir circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through public areas, specific regulations regarding dogs on this particular cycling route are not detailed. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash and be mindful of other cyclists and local wildlife.

faqs.question: What notable sights or landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight and focal point of this route is the Tous Reservoir itself, offering scenic views of the water body and its natural surroundings. The route also passes through charming villages and agricultural landscapes.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other trails for short sections, including "La Defensa del Sur", "El Camí IT5TR6 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana", and "Camí de la Taronja".

faqs.question: Are there any places to stop for food or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in La Pobla Llarga, where you can find cafes and restaurants. Along the circular route itself, options may be limited, so it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks.
